comments.author: @levitabacug1943
comments.text:
The Philippines 🇵🇭 is a country that has more waters than has the land.
That’s a fact.

comments.author: @jasonyoung3690
comments.text: As an American expat living in the Philippines, I can say that most of this video is spot on, except for one glaring falsehood...  there is a large percentage of Filipinos who can't swim!  Most of the ones who live near the sea most definitely can swim much more so than the people who live inland in provincial life where bodies of water are not present, and a lot of folks who live in large metropolises can't swim either.  My wife, who is Filipina, can't swim a lick!  Most of her family can't swim as well.  We recently went on a little rafting trip on a popular local lake surrounded by beautiful limestone escarpments on the shorelines, and the requirement for renting a 'balsa na may kubo,' a raft with a hut, which is the main mode of transport and partying at these lakes on Luzon, is that everyone MUST where a life vest!  I've been swimming all my life, and unless I'm on a fast-moving boat or a great distance from shore, I usually don't wear a life vest!  It was just strange to me, but most who went into the water to 'swim' couldn't swim at all without a vest!

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet4K
comments.text: This is one of the rarest kinds of videos — the kind that doesn’t just entertain you, but stays in your mind long after it’s over. The pacing is patient, the atmosphere is rich, and every moment feels intentional. You can tell this was created with real passion and care. Watching it made me want to continue discovering places like this, and we recently explored a destination on our channel that carries that same haunting sense of mystery but unfolds in even more fascinating ways.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Thank you so much for the incredibly kind words and for noticing the care put into the storytelling!

comments.author: @Indonesiasedangberk
comments.text: The Philippines is a nation of simply overwhelming and almost incomprehensible natural magnificence — an archipelago so vast, so diverse, and so extraordinarily beautiful that it would take a lifetime of exploration to begin to truly know and appreciate all of its wonders.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: That is such a beautiful and accurate way to describe the incredible diversity of these islands!

comments.author: @WorldDiscoveries11
comments.text: Really enjoyed this video. The visuals are beautiful and the way everything is explained makes it really easy and relaxing to watch.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Thank you so much for your kind words! It means a lot to know the visuals and explanations resonated with you.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I couldn't agree more, it truly is a stunning country!

comments.author: @mejoresviajes6688
comments.text:
This video gives a warm glimpse into life in the Philippines, where tropical islands, local traditions, and everyday community life come together beautifully.
Has anyone here ever been to the Philippines? What part would you love to explore first?

comments.author: @WorldDiscovered_Official
comments.text: Watching your documentary made me miss the Philippines so much. The people are so kind and friendly. The food is wonderful too, especially fried chicken with rice. Even though life can still be hard in some places, people always seem so cheerful, always dancing and enjoying the moment. I also love those three wheeled motorbikes there. They are cheap, convenient. Can anyone remind me what the local name for them is?

replies.author: @karenubungen3501
replies.text: Tricycle for motorbikes,pedicab for the 3 wheeled bicycle😊

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I'm so glad my documentary brought back such fond memories of the Philippines for you! It truly is a special place with incredibly kind people, delicious food (fried chicken with rice is a classic!), and that amazing spirit of always enjoying the moment. Those three-wheeled motorbikes are called tricycles!

comments.author: @seanconnery1277
